mainEntity.name: Do I really need a lawyer if the insurance company is already paying me?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Even if they are paying, they often under-calculate benefits or prepare to stop payments as soon as possible. A lawyer protects you from premature return-to-work orders and ensures you get the maximum settlement for long-term disability.
acceptedAnswer.description:
You should ideally contact a lawyer the moment you realize you won't be able to return to your normal job duties. Insurance companies are most aggressive when they face long-term disability payouts. A lawyer will ensure your Average Weekly Wage is calculated correctly (most are under-calculated), stop the adjuster from pressuring you back to work, and select neutral doctors for your evaluations.

If you have already received a denial letter or a 'Notice of Intent to Stop Benefits,' you must call a lawyer immediately.

- Lawyers work on a 'contingency fee,' meaning you pay nothing upfront.
- Fees are capped by state law (usually 20-25%).
- A lawyer often increases the settlement amount by 3-4 times the original offer.

At Just Work Comp Law, we take the stress of the 'paperwork war' off your shoulders so you can focus on healing. If your ability to make a living is at stake, do not fight the insurance company's legal team alone.
